q: Diborane <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> reacts independently with <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> to produce, respectively:
question: Diborane <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> reacts independently with <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> to produce, respectively:
solution:
Diborane reacts with oxygen exothermically to form boric oxide (B2O3)
B2H6+3O2→B2O3+3H2O
Diborane gets readily hydrolysed to give boric acid
B2H6+3H2O→2BOH3+3H2(g)

subject: Chemistry
q: In which one of the following molecules strongest back donation of an electron pair from halide to boron is expected?
question: In which one of the following molecules strongest back donation of an electron pair from halide to boron is expected?
solution:
Due to small size atom show strongest back bonding in BF3.
In case of BF3 (2P-2P back bonding) while in BCl3(2P-3P),BBr3(2P-4P) and BI3(2P-5P) back bonding. So extent of back bonding is maximum in 2P-2P

subject: Chemistry
q: The Lewis acid character of boron tri halides follows the order:
question: The Lewis acid character of boron tri halides follows the order:
solution:
The strength of the acidic character of boron trihalides depends upon π-π back bonding. In boron trihalides, π-π -back bonding occurs due to empty orbital of boron and filled orbitals of halogens.

 

The π-π back bonding is shown maximum byBF3, as the size of B and F are small and comparatively same. Due to this effect tendency of accepting lone-pair of electrons of boron decreases as the size of halogen increases. The order of size of halogens are F < Cl < Br < I . Thus, acidic nature is in order BF3<BCl3<BBr3<Bl3.

X is:
solution:
BCl3


 

Boron react with chlorine gas to produce compound BCl3 which is electron deficient. It does not dimerize because of small boron atom. It react with ammonia to form aduct. In it ammonia act as lewis base and BCl3 act as lewis acid.

subject: Chemistry
q: The amorphous form of silica is:
question: The amorphous form of silica is:
solution:
Kieselguhr, an amorphous form of silica is used in filtration plants.
Quartz is one of the most common minerals in the earth’s crust having a specific crystalline form.
Cristobalite is a high temperature polymorph of silica, having same chemical formula as quartz but distinct crystal structure.
Tridymite is also a high temperature polymorph of silica and occurs as hexagonal crystals.

subject: Chemistry
q: Aqueous solution of which of the following boron compounds will be strongly basic in nature?
question: Aqueous solution of which of the following boron compounds will be strongly basic in nature?
solution:
Na2B4O7 gives H3BO3 and NaOH (strong base) in water.

So, it is a salt of strong base and weak acid. So, its solution will be strongly basic in water. It will have very high pH.

Lithium borohydride and sodium borohydride reacts with water to produce hydrogen.

And diborane act as lewis acid.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List - I with List - II.
List - I Compound List-II Use
(A) Carbon tetrachloride (I) Paint remover
(B) Methylene chloride (II) Refrigerators and air conditioners
(C) DDT (III) Fire extinguisher
(D) Freons (IV) Non Biodegradable insecticide
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Match List - I with List - II.
List - I Compound List-II Use
(A) Carbon tetrachloride (I) Paint remover
(B) Methylene chloride (II) Refrigerators and air conditioners
(C) DDT (III) Fire extinguisher
(D) Freons (IV) Non Biodegradable insecticide
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
CCl4 is used as fire extinguishers under the name of pyrene.
The dense vapours of carbon tetrachloride forms a protective layer on the burning objects and avoids the oxygen or air to come in contact with the fire from the burning objects and provides incombustible vapours.

Chemical paint strippers that include methylene chloride and phenol have been extensively used to remove polymer coatings from metallic substrates. These strippers are inexpensive and remove polymeric organic coatings quickly and easily from a variety of metallic substrates without damage to the substrate.

Dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane (DDT) is an organic halogen that acts by killing insects rapidly upon contact. DDT is harm to all biota of the planet as it is non-biodegradable, and tends to become more concentrated as it moves up the food chain.

The freons are simple fluorinated and aliphatic organic compounds that contain fluorine, carbon, hydrogen, chlorine, bromine etc. These are used as the refrigerants and propellants. Chlorofluorocarbons, Hydrochlorofluorocarbons are the types of freons. They have a low boiling point and low suface tension due to which they are used in refrigerators, air conditioners etc. However, they undergo photo-dissociation when exposed to the UV rays, releasing a reactive chlorine ion that is capable of reacting with ozone. This reaction is responsible for the depletion of the ozone in the stratosphere. Hence, the correct answer is 'Freon'.

Hence, CCl4 used in fire extinguisher. CH2Cl2 used as paint remover. Freons used in refrigerator and AC.DDT used as non Biodegradable insecticide.

question:
Consider the following elements $\mathrm{In}, \mathrm{Tl}, \mathrm{Al}, \mathrm{Pb}, \mathrm{Sn}$ and Ge .
The most stable oxidation states of elements with highest and lowest first ionisation enthalpies, respectively, are
solution:
Among $\mathrm{Al}, \mathrm{In}, \mathrm{Tl}, \mathrm{Ge}, \mathrm{Sn}, \mathrm{Pb}$, the metal having highest $\mathrm{IE}_1$ is Ge and lowest $\mathrm{IE}_1$ is In .
Most stable oxidation state of Ge is +4 and In is +3 .

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A : Boric acid is a weak acid

Reason R : Boric acid is not able to release H+ ion on its own. It receives OH- ion from water and releases H+ ion.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
question:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A : Boric acid is a weak acid

Reason R : Boric acid is not able to release H+ ion on its own. It receives OH- ion from water and releases H+ ion.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
options: Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> are correct and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> are correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is NOT the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is not correct

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is not correct but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is correct
solution: Boric acid is a weak acid because it does not completely ionize in water or other aqueous solution. It is not able to release H+ ions on its own because firstly it receives hydroxide ions OH- from water molecule in order to complete its octet and then it releases H+ ions.

subject: Chemistry
q:
The correct statements from the following are :
A. Ionic radii of trivalent cations of group 13 elements decreases down the group.
B. Electronegativity of group 13 elements decreases down the group.
C. Among the group 13 elements, Boron has highest first ionisation enthalpy.
D. The trichloride and triiodide of group 13 elements are covalent in nature.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
question:
The correct statements from the following are :
A. Ionic radii of trivalent cations of group 13 elements decreases down the group.
B. Electronegativity of group 13 elements decreases down the group.
C. Among the group 13 elements, Boron has highest first ionisation enthalpy.
D. The trichloride and triiodide of group 13 elements are covalent in nature.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
solution:
Analyzing statements about Group 13 elements (B, Al, Ga, In, Tl):
A. Ionic radii of M³⁺ cations: Down a group, more electron shells are added, so ionic radii increase, not decrease. FALSE
B. Electronegativity: Generally decreases down the group, though Ga shows an anomaly with higher electronegativity than Al. The statement is approximately true but has exceptions.
C. First ionization enthalpy: B (800.6 kJ/mol) is highest due to smallest size and highest nuclear charge density. Al (577.5), Ga (579), In (558), Tl (589). TRUE
D. Trichlorides and triodides: BCl₃, AlCl₃, GaCl₃, etc. are all covalent compounds (electron-deficient). BI₃, AlI₃, GaI₃, etc. are also covalent (I⁻ is large and polarizable). TRUE
The clearly correct statements are C and D.

q: Boron cannot form which one of the following anions?
question: Boron cannot form which one of the following anions?
solution:
As Boron has only four orbitals in the valence shell ( i.e. $2 s, 2 p_x, 2 p_y \& 2 p_z$ ) it can show a maximum valency of four only.
Therefore $\left[\mathrm{BF}_6\right]^{3-}$ is not possible

q: The ratio of number of water molecules in Mohr's salt and potash alum is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>.</mo></math> (Integer answer)

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I : The chlorides of Be and Al have Cl-bridged structure. Both are soluble in organic solvents and act as Lewis bases.

Statement II: Hydroxides of Be and Al dissolve in excess alkali to give beryllate and aluminate ions. In the light of the above statements. Choose the correct answer from the options given below.
question:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I : The chlorides of Be and Al have Cl-bridged structure. Both are soluble in organic solvents and act as Lewis bases.

Statement II: Hydroxides of Be and Al dissolve in excess alkali to give beryllate and aluminate ions. In the light of the above statements. Choose the correct answer from the options given below.
options: Both statement I and Statement II are true
Both statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
The chlorides of both beryllium and aluminium have Cl– bridged chloride structure in vapour phase. Both the chlorides are soluble in organic solvents and are strong Lewis acids. They are used as Friedel Craft catalysts.

Be2Cl4 is lewis acid and Al2Cl6 has complete octet.

Be and Al are amphoteric metals therefore dissolve in acid as well as alkaline solution and form beryllate and aluminate ions in excess alkali.

 Beryllium hydroxide dissolves in excess of alkali to give a beryllate ion,[Be(OH)4 ]2– just as aluminium hydroxide gives aluminate ion, [AlH2O2(OH)4]-

subject: Chemistry
q:
The geometry around boron in the product 'B' formed from the following reaction is

BF3+NaH⟶450 KA+NaF

A+NMe3→B
question:
The geometry around boron in the product 'B' formed from the following reaction is

BF3+NaH⟶450 KA+NaF

A+NMe3→B
solution:
2BF3+6NaH→B2H6A+6NaF

In this reaction hydride ions replace the fluoride ions. and diborane is formed, which is a Lewis acid due to incomplete octet. It breaks symmetrically with bases like tertiary amines.

B2H6+2Me3N→2Me3N+BBH3- 

In this product geometry around Boron will be tetrahedral. And it will have sp3 hybridisation.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Consider the following reactions
$\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{~B}_{4} \mathrm{O}_{7} \xrightarrow{\Delta} 2 \mathrm{X}+\mathrm{Y}$
$\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}+\mathrm{Y} \xrightarrow{\text { Non-Luminous flame }} \mathrm{Z}+\mathrm{SO}_{3}$
$2 \mathrm{Z}+2 \mathrm{X}+$ Carbon $\xrightarrow{\text { Luminous flame }} 2 \mathrm{Q}+\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{~B}_{4} \mathrm{O}_{7}+\mathrm{CO}$
The oxidation states of Cu in Z and Q, respectively are :
question:
Consider the following reactions
$\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{~B}_{4} \mathrm{O}_{7} \xrightarrow{\Delta} 2 \mathrm{X}+\mathrm{Y}$
$\mathrm{CuSO}_{4}+\mathrm{Y} \xrightarrow{\text { Non-Luminous flame }} \mathrm{Z}+\mathrm{SO}_{3}$
$2 \mathrm{Z}+2 \mathrm{X}+$ Carbon $\xrightarrow{\text { Luminous flame }} 2 \mathrm{Q}+\mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{~B}_{4} \mathrm{O}_{7}+\mathrm{CO}$
The oxidation states of Cu in Z and Q, respectively are :
solution:
The given reactions describe the Borax Bead Test.
When borax ($Na_2B_4O_7 \cdot 10H_2O$) is heated, it first loses water and then melts to form a transparent glassy bead consisting of sodium metaborate ($NaBO_2$) and boric anhydride ($B_2O_3$).
$Na_2B_4O_7 \xrightarrow{\Delta} 2NaBO_2 (X) + B_2O_3 (Y)$
In the non-luminous (oxidizing) flame, $B_2O_3$ reacts with $CuSO_4$ to form copper(II) metaborate ($Z$), which is blue in color.
$CuSO_4 + B_2O_3 \xrightarrow{Non-Luminous} Cu(BO_2)_2 (Z) + SO_3$
In $Cu(BO_2)_2$, the oxidation state of $Cu$ is $+2$.
In the luminous (reducing) flame, the copper(II) metaborate is reduced by carbon to copper(I) metaborate ($Q$), which is colorless/red.
$2Cu(BO_2)_2 (Z) + 2NaBO_2 (X) + C \xrightarrow{Luminous} 2CuBO_2 (Q) + Na_2B_4O_7 + CO$
In $CuBO_2$, the oxidation state of $Cu$ is $+1$.
Therefore, the oxidation states of $Cu$ in $Z$ and $Q$ are $+2$ and $+1$ respectively.

question: The relative stability of +1 oxidation state of group 13 elements follows the order

state increases down the group. Thus, correct order of stability is $\mathrm{Al}  < \mathrm{Ga} < \mathrm{In} < \mathrm{Tl}$

question: Example of a three-dimensional silicate is :

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements :
Statement I : The number of pairs among $\left[\mathrm{SiO}_{2}, \mathrm{CO}_{2}\right],\left[\mathrm{SnO}, \mathrm{SnO}_{2}\right],\left[\mathrm{PbO}, \mathrm{PbO}_{2}\right]$ and $\left[\mathrm{GeO}, \mathrm{GeO}_{2}\right]$, which contain oxides that are both amphoteric is 2.
Statement II: $\mathrm{BF}_{3}$ is an electron deficient molecule, can act as a Lewis acid, forms adduct with $\mathrm{NH}_{3}$ and has a trigonal planar geometry.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
question:
Given below are two statements :
Statement I : The number of pairs among $\left[\mathrm{SiO}_{2}, \mathrm{CO}_{2}\right],\left[\mathrm{SnO}, \mathrm{SnO}_{2}\right],\left[\mathrm{PbO}, \mathrm{PbO}_{2}\right]$ and $\left[\mathrm{GeO}, \mathrm{GeO}_{2}\right]$, which contain oxides that are both amphoteric is 2.
Statement II: $\mathrm{BF}_{3}$ is an electron deficient molecule, can act as a Lewis acid, forms adduct with $\mathrm{NH}_{3}$ and has a trigonal planar geometry.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
Statement I: Checking amphoteric nature of oxide pairs:
$[SiO_2, CO_2]$: Both acidic (non-metal oxides)
$[SnO, SnO_2]$: Both amphoteric
$[PbO, PbO_2]$: $PbO$ is amphoteric but $PbO_2$ is predominantly acidic/oxidizing
$[GeO, GeO_2]$: Both amphoteric (Ge is metalloid)
2 pairs have both oxides amphoteric. Statement I is true.
Statement II: $BF_3$ has B with 6 valence electrons (electron deficient), acts as Lewis acid, forms $BF_3 \cdot NH_3$ adduct, has $sp^2$ hybridization giving trigonal planar geometry. Statement II is true.

subject: Chemistry
q: The number of neutrons present in the more abundant isotope of boron is ' \(x\) '. Amorphous boron upon heating with air forms a product, in which the oxidation state of boron is ' \(y\) '. The value of \(x+y\) is _______
question: The number of neutrons present in the more abundant isotope of boron is ' \(x\) '. Amorphous boron upon heating with air forms a product, in which the oxidation state of boron is ' \(y\) '. The value of \(x+y\) is _______
solution:
More abundant isotope $=\mathrm{B}^{11}$
[Number of neutrons $=6$ ]
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{x}=6 \\
& \mathrm{~B}+\mathrm{O}_2 \rightarrow \mathrm{B}_2 \mathrm{O}_3
\end{aligned}$
Oxidation state of $\mathrm{B}$ in $\mathrm{B}_2 \mathrm{O}_3=+3$
So, $y=3$
Hence $x+y=9$

solution:
Diborane is prepared by the reaction of NaBH4 with I2. 2NaBH4+I2→B2H6+2NaI+H2
In diborane, B is sp3 hybridized. It is non-planar molecule and it has two 3c-2e- bonds.

subject: Chemistry
q: The number of <math><mn>2</mn></math>-centre-<math><mn>2</mn></math>-electron and <math><mn>3</mn></math>-centre-<math><mn>2</mn></math>-electron bonds in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>6</mn></msub></math>, respectively, are:
question: The number of <math><mn>2</mn></math>-centre-<math><mn>2</mn></math>-electron and <math><mn>3</mn></math>-centre-<math><mn>2</mn></math>-electron bonds in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>6</mn></msub></math>, respectively, are:
solution: B2H6 has 2 bridge bonds 3C-2e- and 4 terminal bonds 2C-2e-.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Identify the correct statement for B2H6 from those given below.

(A) In B2H6, all B-H bonds are equivalent.

(B) In B2H6, there are four 3-centre- 2-electron bonds.

(C) B2H6 is a Lewis acid.

(D) B2H6 can be synthesized from both BF3 and NaBH4.

(E) B2H6 is a planar molecule.

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
question:
Identify the correct statement for B2H6 from those given below.

(A) In B2H6, all B-H bonds are equivalent.

(B) In B2H6, there are four 3-centre- 2-electron bonds.

(C) B2H6 is a Lewis acid.

(D) B2H6 can be synthesized from both BF3 and NaBH4.

(E) B2H6 is a planar molecule.

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
solution:
B2H6 have four 2c-2e bonds and two 3c-2e bonds. Bridging bonds have larger bond length than terminal bonds. Angle between terminal bonds is more than angle between bridging bonds if all 4 terminal bonds are in one plane then bridging bonds are in perpendicular plane.

3NaBH4+4BF3⟶ether450 K3NaBF4+2B2H6

Therefore we can say that only statement (C) and (D) are correct.
